commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can address the remaining partitioner edge case without a product or ownership decision.; Needs attention: The patch is supply-chain clean, but the remaining partitioner edge case can still expose hidden model reasoning in user-facing chat delivery.; Review finding: [P2] Keep unclosed reasoning tags hidden in visible streams — src/shared/text/reasoning-tag-text-partitioner.ts:14 (sha=692aa83b4e1662158e2853b7cb2cb9ecd5e6034e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR expands assistant-text sanitization, embedded chat streaming filters, and focused tests so `<reasoning>` and `<antml:reasoning>` hidden-control blocks are stripped while reasoning-prefixed visible tags are preserved.

PR surface: Source +74, Tests +173. Total +247 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: OpenAI-compatible streamed content without mirrored reasoning uses `pushVisible()`, and the partitioner’s final visible-mode recovery can emit unclosed hidden-region text as `kind: "text"`. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can address the remaining partitioner edge case without a product or ownership decision.

target.title: fix(agents): strip reasoning tags from chat replies
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89473.

- Treat `<reasoning>` and `<antml:reasoning>` as reasoning-control tags in the shared final assistant text sanitizer.
- Align the embedded stream/block-reply tag matcher, message recompute trigger, and stream partitioner with the same exact tag-name boundary so visible XML-ish tags such as `<reasoning-step>` and `<antml:reasoning.step>` stay visible.
- Treat self-closing reasoning tags such as `<reasoning/>` as empty hidden-control blocks so they do not hide or stream the following visible text.
- Add focused sanitizer, partitioner, embedded extraction, and subscriber regressions for regular, namespaced, split, attribute-bearing, visible-prefix, and self-closing tags.

## Root Cause

The OpenAI-compatible stream partitioner already recognized `reasoning` tags, but the final sanitizer and embedded stream delivery matchers only covered think/thinking/thought variants. That left `<reasoning>...</reasoning>` text able to survive final assistant extraction or chat block-reply delivery. Split opener fragments could leak when the stream buffered only the older tag names, and attribute-bearing openers could still be missed by the embedded stream scanner even though the shared sanitizer accepted attributes.

The first attribute-support patch used `\b[^<>]*>` as the tag tail. That accepted visible tag names that merely started with `reasoning`, such as `<reasoning-step>`, because `\b` matches before `-` and `.`. The current patch now requires the next character after the recognized control tag name to be whitespace, `/`, or `>`.

Self-closing open tags were also being treated as normal openers. For `<reasoning/>Visible`, that could hide the visible suffix in text sanitizers or stream `Visible` as reasoning text in embedded reasoning extraction.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Assistant-visible final text and embedded chat block-reply streaming now strip hidden `<reasoning>` and `<antml:reasoning>` blocks through the same reasoning-tag taxonomy used for existing think/thinking tags, including when opening tags are split across stream chunks, carry attributes, or are self-closing. Visible non-control tags such as `<reasoning-step>` remain user-visible.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch at `692aa83b4e1662158e2853b7cb2cb9ecd5e6034e`, running the source shared sanitizer, embedded assistant extraction, embedded reasoning stream extraction, stream partitioner, and focused `subscribeEmbeddedAgentSession` block-reply regression tests with `blockReplyBreak: "text_end"`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local `pnpm exec tsx --eval` command that imported `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.ts`, and `src/shared/text/reasoning-tag-text-partitioner.ts` and exercised `<reasoning/>Visible` plus `<reasoning-step>show this</reasoning-step>`. Also ran the focused Vitest files covering shared sanitizer behavior, the stream partitioner, embedded extraction/reasoning stream behavior, split/attribute-bearing block replies, visible `reasoning-step` block replies, and self-closing block replies.

Evidence after fix: Console output from the direct source probe:

```json
{
  "sanitizerSelfClosing": "Visible",
  "sanitizerPrefix": "<reasoning-step>show this</reasoning-step>",
  "visibleExtractionSelfClosing": "Visible",
  "reasoningStreamSelfClosing": "",
  "partitionerSelfClosing": [
    {
      "kind": "text",
      "text": "Visible"
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: The visible suffix after `<reasoning/>` remains visible, self-closing tags emit no reasoning stream text, and visible `reasoning-step` content is preserved. Focused subscriber tests also show attribute-bearing reasoning blocks are withheld until the close tag arrives and only the visible final reply is emitted.

What was not tested: Full repository suite, broad changed checks, GitHub CI beyond the checks GitHub ran for this PR, and live MiniMax/LiteLLM reproduction.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec tsx --eval '...'` direct source proof above
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tag-text-partitioner.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 5 files / 237 tests passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tag-text-partitioner.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tag-text-partitioner.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed
target.author: monkeyleet
